key_items: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
Why is Bitcoin falling despite pro-crypto Kevin Warsh becoming Fed chair? -- Rising short-term bond yields and Warsh's hawkish comments in the past are reviving fears of a December rate hike
Analysts forecast a Bitcoin crash to the $60K level, revisiting 2026 low -- The price of Bitcoin is about $75,800 at the time of publication, a nearly 40% decrease from the all-time high
reasoning: Multiple bearish pressures converge: Bitcoin has broken below $75K with $1B+ in liquidations, bond yields rising with anticipated Fed rate hikes contradicting earlier pro-crypto sentiment, and major institutional capitulation (Mark Cuban, Trump Media selling). The macro environment has shifted from rate-cut expectations to hawkish Fed positioning, directly headwinds for risk assets. Positive regulation signals (national Bitcoin reserve proposals) and institutional adoption (BofA picking Bitcoin) are outweighed by liquidation cascades and technical breakdown below key support.
risks: Continued liquidations could cascade below $75K toward analyst targets of $60K if key support fails and Fed maintains hawkish guidance through December rate hike cycle.
